Risk Description: Primary business subject matter expert may be unavailable for requirements workshops due to competing operational priorities.
Mitigation Action: Identify and brief a backup SME for each domain. Schedule workshops 3 weeks in advance. Include SME availability as a project dependency in the charter.
Contingency Plan: Reschedule workshop with backup SME. Extend requirements phase by 1 week if necessary.
Notes: SME confirmed for Phase 1 workshops. Phase 2 SME TBC.

Risk Description: Stakeholders may request additional features or functionality beyond the agreed project scope during development or UAT phases.
Mitigation Action: Implement formal change control process. All scope changes require written change request with impact assessment and sponsor approval before work begins.
Contingency Plan: Assess impact of requested change on timeline and budget. Present options to sponsor. Defer to Phase 2 if not approved.
Notes: Change control process defined in PMP. Stakeholders briefed at kickoff.

Risk Description: External vendor may not deliver contracted components on schedule due to resource constraints or technical issues on their side.
Mitigation Action: Include delivery milestones with financial penalties in vendor contract. Conduct bi-weekly vendor delivery check-ins. Maintain milestone tracking dashboard.
Contingency Plan: Activate escalation clause in vendor contract. Assess feasibility of internal resource backfill. Notify sponsor immediately.
Notes: Vendor contract under review by legal.

Risk Description: Integration between the new system and existing ERP/legacy systems may prove more complex than anticipated during discovery.
Mitigation Action: Conduct technical spike in discovery phase to validate integration approach. Engage technical architect early. Allocate 15% contingency buffer for technical work.
Contingency Plan: Engage specialist integration resource. Assess phased integration approach if full integration not feasible in timeline.

Risk Description: End users may resist transitioning from familiar manual processes to the new system resulting in low adoption rates post go-live.
Mitigation Action: Engage change management stream from project initiation. Involve end users in requirements and UAT. Develop training materials 6 weeks before go-live. Identify and activate change champions in each department.
Contingency Plan: Provide additional 1:1 training sessions. Extend hypercare support period from 2 to 4 weeks post go-live.
Notes: Change champion network identified. Training plan in development.

Risk Description: Unforeseen technical complexity or scope changes may cause the project to exceed its approved budget.
Mitigation Action: Maintain a 15% contingency reserve. Conduct monthly budget vs. actual reviews. Require sponsor approval for any expenditure exceeding $10K above planned.
Contingency Plan: Present options to sponsor: defer scope to Phase 2 or request budget amendment. Document formally.

Risk Description: Handling of personal data (employee or student records) in the new system may inadvertently violate FIPPA/PIPEDA requirements if not properly governed.
Mitigation Action: Engage Privacy Officer in requirements phase. Conduct Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) before go-live. Ensure data classification applied to all personal data fields.
Contingency Plan: Suspend go-live pending privacy remediation. Engage legal and privacy teams immediately.

Risk Description: A key project team member may leave the organization during the project causing knowledge loss and delivery impact.
Mitigation Action: Maintain up-to-date project documentation and knowledge base. Cross-train team members on critical tasks. Ensure no single point of failure for specialized knowledge.
Contingency Plan: Backfill resource through HR or engage external contractor. Allow 2-week knowledge transfer period.

Risk Description: Historical data migration from legacy system to new platform may result in data quality issues or data loss.
Mitigation Action: Develop and execute a data migration plan with validation checks. Run parallel processing for 4 weeks post go-live. Conduct data reconciliation report before and after migration.
Contingency Plan: Roll back to legacy system if critical data integrity issues found. Engage data team for emergency remediation.

Risk Description: Executive sponsor may change roles or leave the organization during the project resulting in loss of strategic support and decision-making authority.
Mitigation Action: Document all sponsor decisions and approvals formally. Brief incoming sponsor comprehensively. Identify a secondary executive who can provide interim support.
Contingency Plan: Conduct immediate sponsor onboarding session. Re-present project charter and status to new sponsor for re-confirmation.
